title: Pre-allocate Vec capacity to avoid incremental reallocation
description: A Vec built by repeated push() in a loop of known/estimable length reallocates and memcpys its buffer O(log n) times as it grows. Reserving capacity up front turns that into a single allocation.

trigger_condition.explanation: Flag a `Vec::new()` (or `vec![]`) bound to a mutable binding that is subsequently grown with push() inside a loop whose iteration count is derivable from a slice/iterator length. The ast_grep_pattern matches the real call node via tree-sitter, so occurrences in comments/strings are ignored (unlike the regex fallback).

references.title: The Rust Performance Book — Heap Allocations
